titleStmt.title: SII 32, part 2, no. 47: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 47. (A.R. No. 534 of 1905.) TIRUVEḶḶAṞAI, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the north base, second pillar in the rock-cut cave, Puṇḍarikāksha-perumāḷ temple.

Incomplete and damaged. It is stated that it is a record of a gift of [2]0 kaḻañju of gold weighed by Tiruveḷḷaṟaikal by Īrāyiraṉdēvi-Ammaṉār, the wife of the lord who died on the bank of an elephant (uḍaiyār āṉai = mēṟ = ṟuñjiṉār) in order to provide food offerings with 4 nāḻi of rice on the occasion of ashṭami, on the new moon, and full moon days and on saṅkrānti (every month ?) to god Śrī Kṛishṇa and goddess Rukmiṇi in the big temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai. The lady also seems to have deposited gold for a lamp.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. III. No. 132.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . kopparakesaripanmaṟkki yāṇṭu 8 Āvatu tiruveḷḷaṟai periya śrī
koyilil śrīkraṣṇarkkum śrīrukmiṇippirāṭṭiyārkkum Uṭaiyār Āṉaimeṟṟu
ñciṉār deviyār IrāyiraṉRead īrāyiraṉṟeviyammaṉār kuṭuttapaṭi Eḻutiyatu śrīkraṣṇarkku
Iraṇṭu vāvum Aṣṭamiyum saṅgrān=tiyumRead saṅkrāIn nāḷḷoṉṟaikku nāṉāḻi (nāṉāḻi) Arici
yāl AmutupaṭimmutuRead amutupaṭiceyvikka vaitta poṉ tiruveḷḷaṟai kallāl mutala
rupatiṉ kaḻañcum viḷakkoṉṟukku vaitta poṉ tiruveḷḷaṟai kallāl
Digital edition of SII 32, part 2, no. 47 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
